title: Crepis mira
taxonomy: Crepis mira is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants that belong to the order Asterales. intro: Crepis mira, also known as the rare or wonder hawk's beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Crepis mira include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
